title: rubus subjectus
intro: Rubus subjectus, also known as the creeping raspberry, is a type of wild raspberry native to eastern North America. Its medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its high content of antioxidants, anthocyanins, and ellagic acid, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anticancer properties. The health benefits of Rubus subjectus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus subjectus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin irritations.
